titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul Hindemith Oral History Collection
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Anne Rhodes

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday May 24, 2020 at 00:00

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Paul Hindemith Oral History consists of seventy-five interviews with students, performers, colleagues, and friends of the celebrated German composer. The Hindemith oral history complements and gives added dimension to the Paul Hindemith Collection of personal papers and manuscripts in the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library at Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research. Streaming access to sound recordings in this collection is available when connected to the Yale network, or otherwise by request.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: OHI, Paul Hindemith Oral History Collection, Oral History of American Music, in the Music Library of Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged alphabetically by surname of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Rights and permissions for OHAM materials vary between individual interviews. Researchers wishing to publish or broadcast OHAM materials are responsible for obtaining direct authorization from copyright holders: generally, the interviewees or their estates.
